title: Peloton Interactive (PTON) Q1 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Peloton Q1 2023 Earnings Call Summary. Key Highlights Financial Performance: The company achieved significant progress on cost structure and free cash flow reduction. Management maintained their goal of reaching near breakeven free cash flow in the second half of fiscal 2023, with CFO Liz Coddington stating they remain on track despite acknowledging risks. Growth Initiatives: CEO Barry McCarthy outlined several strategic priorities. Fitness-as-a-Service (FaaS) rental model is performing strongly with approximately 175 signups daily. McCarthy indicated FaaS could represent 30% or more of new subscribers, though the revenue profile differs from core business due to rental income timing. Retail Expansion: The company launched partnerships with Amazon and Dick's Sporting Goods. Amazon has outperformed our expectations, though performance varies by channel and model (wholesale vs. dropship). Digital App Relaunch: Plans include tiered pricing and targeting 100 million users, compared to the current 1 million maximum historically achieved. Row Product: Despite inventory constraints, the new rower has received strong reception and rolled out to all showrooms. Operational Changes: Management eliminated approximately 500 positions to complete cost restructuring, with McCarthy emphasizing We are done with layoffs as the company shifts toward growth-focused operations. Guidance: Q2 guidance projects 30,000 subscriber additions, with expectations of improved gross margins (around 36% overall) as logistics costs decline.
